Description: Single fixed value with no variation
Use_Cases: Default/baseline values where no uncertainty exists
Lead times with symmetric variation around mean
Lead times/durations that cannot be negative and have long tail

Description: Flat distribution - all values equally likely
Use_Cases: Capacity with unknown but bounded variation
Expert estimates with most likely value
Time between failures/events (shelf life aging)
Processing times with minimum threshold
Equipment reliability/failure modeling
Parameter_1_Description: Shape parameter (k) - <1 decreasing failure rate >1 increasing

Use_Cases: Yield percentages/quality rates (naturally bounded 0-100%)
Parameter_1_Description: Alpha shape parameter (α) - controls left skew
Parameter_2_Description: Beta shape parameter (β) - controls right skew

Use_Cases: Discrete demand counts per period (0 1 2 3...)
Parameter_1_Description: Average rate (λ) - mean events per period

Description: Discrete distribution of successes in n trials
Use_Cases: Count of defective items in batch (k successes in n trials)
Demand with more variation than Poisson can capture
Historical data or expert judgment with specific scenarios
Parameter_2_Description: Probability of each value (must sum to 1.0)

Use_Cases: Historical data samples used directly
Complex distributions not matching standard forms
Parameter_2_Description: Cumulative probabilities at each breakpoint (0 to 1)

Use_Cases: Bimodal scenarios (e.g. normal ops vs disruption)
Parameter_1_Description: List of component distributions (each with type and params)
Parameter_2_Description: Weight of each component (must sum to 1.0)

Use_Cases: Normally distributed but with physical constraints
Right-skewed with enforced limits

Description: Discrete uniform - integer values equally likely
Use_Cases: Random integer selection with no preference
Failure modes supplier selection discrete scenarios
Parameter_2_Description: Probability of each category (must sum to 1.0)
